title: hoya heuschkeliana subsp. marionii
intro: Hoya heuschkeliana subsp. marionii, also known as the Wax Heart, is a flowering plant species in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its waxy, heart-shaped leaves and fragrant, star-shaped flowers. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Hoya heuschkeliana subsp. marionii include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water, and are used in various parts of the world to treat a range of ailments.
